mission.title: lima: Set instance name while starting
mission.description: Set instance name while starting. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current lima setup
steps.description:
Verify your lima version and configuration:
```bash
limactl --version
```
This feature requires a working lima installation.

steps.title: Review lima configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant lima configuration:
Running `limactl start foo.yml` automatically sets the instance name to `foo`. It would be nice to be able to set a name different than the file name. Ex. running `limactl start foo.yml --name bar` would create an instance named `bar`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Set instance name while starting
steps.description:
> * I guess that is the same as what you have in mind too with NAME attribute/arg to start along with filename/template URL

Yes, this sounds pretty similar. My suggestion would be to have both `--from` and `--from-default` options. `--from` would require an argument (URL, filename),
```yaml
> limactl start bar --from examples/foo.yaml
>
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Set instance name while starting" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> * I guess that is the same as what you have in mind too with NAME attribute/arg to start along with filename/template URL

Yes, this sounds pretty similar. My suggestion would be to have both `--from` and `--from-default` options.

prerequisites.description: A working lima installation or development environment.
